summary: Round-2 battery-backstop implementation: phases 1, 2, 3, 5 complete, build-verified, and committed (backstop timer c72c4cf, GNOME battery timeout 2386332, inhibitor tether 29fdd1f, docs 09eb586). Both plan-flagged empirical dependencies verified: hook-shell $PPID IS the owning claude process (probe), and the tail --pid tether releases the inhibitor within ~1s of owner kill -9 (isolated test). Phase 4 (activation + runtime verification) pending user: interactive sudo unavailable and agents must not switch/suspend.

artifacts.summary: battery-suspend-backstop service + timer: BAT* poll every 60s, systemctl suspend -i at <=10% Discharging, inhibitor-proof
SessionStart inhibitor payload sleep infinity -> tail --pid=$PPID tether + stale-pidfile reap; SessionEnd unchanged
Battery backstop bullet, 60-min battery idle-suspend with inhibitor caveat, accepted limitation, stale 15-minute claims removed
Round-2 implementation summary with user activation checklist and one-time stale-pidfile cleanup command

completion_data.completion_summary: Battery-level suspend backstop implemented and build-verified: root systemd timer suspends at <=10% while discharging via systemctl suspend -i (bypasses all sleep inhibitors), GNOME battery idle-suspend raised to 60 minutes with explicit suspend type, Claude session inhibitors tethered to their owning process so crashed sessions release their locks. Activation (sudo nixos-rebuild switch + reboot + runtime checks) pending user.

partial_progress.details: Phases 1,2,3,5 [COMPLETED]; Phase 4 [BLOCKED] pending-user-activation (interactive sudo unavailable; suspend tests are user-performed)

next_steps: User: sudo nixos-rebuild switch --flake .#hamsa, reboot, then run the Phase 4 checklist in summaries/02_battery-level-backstop-summary.md (incl. the safe suspend vs suspend -i A/B test and the scratch-session kill-9 tether check).
